reference_standard: Amyloid-PET positivity (visual read or Centiloid >24) primary; CSF Aβ42/40 ratio alternative
population: Cognitively impaired adults under evaluation (MCI / dementia work-up); cognitively unimpaired older adults secondary

primary_tier.raw_quote: In the BioFINDER-2 cohort discriminating AD dementia from non-AD neurodegenerative disorders (n=301), plasma p-tau217 had a higher AUC (0.96; 95% CI 0.93-0.98) than plasma p-tau181 or p-tau231; sensitivity for AD vs non-AD neurodegenerative was 88% (95% CI 81%-93%) at specificity 93% (95% CI 88%-96%) using the optimal threshold. Discrimination of amyloid-PET-positive vs amyloid-PET-negative was similar.

ALZpath plasma p-tau217 immunoassay was evaluated against amyloid-PET visual read across three cohorts (TRIAD n=268, BioFINDER-2 n=148, Sant Pau n=170; total N=586). Diagnostic performance for amyloid-PET positivity was AUC 0.94 (95% CI 0.92-0.96); at the manufacturer cutoff sensitivity was 89.7% and specificity 90.1%. A two-cutoff approach with an intermediate zone reduced confirmatory CSF/PET need by ~80% in the combined cohort.

primary_tier.country: USA (Mayo Clinic Study of Aging, Olmsted MN)
primary_tier.raw_quote: Among 194 MCSA participants with concurrent plasma p-tau217 (Lilly MSD) and amyloid-PET (Pittsburgh Compound B, SUVR threshold), plasma p-tau217 discriminated amyloid-PET-positive from amyloid-PET-negative with AUC 0.83 (95% CI 0.77-0.89). At the Youden-optimal cutoff, sensitivity was 73% and specificity 75%. Discrimination was lower than in memory-clinic cohorts because the population-based sample includes more low-amyloid CU older adults and fewer high-amyloid AD-dementia cases at the high end of the distribution.

In the BioFINDER-1 cohort (n=211 with concurrent plasma p-tau and amyloid-PET, including MCI and AD-dementia), plasma p-tau217 (Lilly MSD prototype assay) discriminated amyloid-PET-positive from amyloid-PET-negative with AUC 0.92 (95% CI 0.88-0.95), exceeding plasma p-tau181 (AUC 0.86). Sensitivity at the pre-specified cutoff was 86%, specificity 89%. Plasma p-tau217 also tracked longitudinal amyloid accumulation and predicted progression to AD dementia.

sensitivity_tier_added.raw_quote: We evaluated a two-step plasma p-tau217 workflow in BioFINDER-2 (n=348) and Wisconsin ADRC (n=128) using CSF Aβ42/40 as the reference. Single-cutoff sensitivity was 88% and specificity 89% in the combined cohort. With a two-cutoff approach, ~80% of participants were classified directly by plasma p-tau217 alone, restricting confirmatory CSF/PET to ~20% with intermediate values; among those classified by plasma alone, accuracy versus the CSF reference was >95%.

_engine_expected_flags.note: These are documentation only - the engine recomputes at page-load
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_combined: REML or FE bivariate (k=5 - boundary case)
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_primary: REML or FE bivariate (k=4 - boundary case)
heterogeneity_axes: clinical_status (MCI vs dementia vs CU vs mixed)
